original_query: SELECT "EMP"."SAL" + "BONUS"."COMM", SUM("BONUS"."SAL" + "BONUS"."SAL" + 100) OVER (PARTITION BY "BONUS"."JOB" RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) FROM "EMP" INNER JOIN "BONUS" ON "EMP"."ENAME" = "BONUS"."ENAME" AND "EMP"."DEPTNO" = 10
rewritten_query:
SELECT "EMP"."SAL" + "BONUS"."COMM",
  SUM(2 * "BONUS"."SAL" + 100) OVER (PARTITION BY "BONUS"."JOB" ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) 
FROM "EMP" 
INNER JOIN "BONUS" 
  ON "EMP"."ENAME" = "BONUS"."ENAME" 
WHERE "EMP"."DEPTNO" = 10;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Simplified the redundant expression 'BONUS.SAL + BONUS.SAL + 100' to '2 * BONUS.SAL + 100' to reduce computation.
Changed window frame specification from RANGE to ROWS for potential performance improvement in the window function.
Moved the filter condition 'EMP.DEPTNO = 10' from the JOIN clause to the WHERE clause for better readability while maintaining the same execution plan.
